an4.34:1.1: “Monks, there are these four supreme objects of confidence.
an4.34:1.3: “Among whatever beings there may be—footless, two-footed, four-footed, many footed; with form or formless; percipient, non-percipient, neither percipient nor non-percipient—the Tathāgata, worthy & rightly self-awakened, is considered supreme.
an4.34:1.4: Those who have confidence in the Awakened One have confidence in what is supreme;
an4.34:1.5: and for those with confidence in the supreme, supreme is the result.
an4.34:2.1: “Among whatever fabricated qualities there may be, the noble eightfold path is considered supreme.
an4.34:2.2: Those who have confidence in the noble eightfold path have confidence in what is supreme;
an4.34:2.3: and for those with confidence in the supreme, supreme is the result.
an4.34:3.1: “Among whatever dhammas there may be, fabricated or unfabricated, dispassion—the subduing of intoxication, the elimination of thirst, the uprooting of attachment, the breaking of the round, the destruction of craving, dispassion, cessation, the realization of unbinding—is considered supreme.
an4.34:3.2: Those who have confidence in the dhamma of dispassion have confidence in what is supreme;
an4.34:3.3: and for those with confidence in the supreme, supreme is the result.
an4.34:4.1: “Among whatever communities or groups there may be, the Saṅgha of the Tathāgata’s disciples is considered supreme—i.e., the four [groups of noble disciples] when taken as pairs, the eight when taken as persons.
an4.34:4.2: Those who have confidence in the Saṅgha have confidence in what is supreme;
an4.34:4.3: and for those with confidence in the supreme, supreme will be the result.
an4.34:4.4: “These, monks, are the four supreme objects of confidence.”
an4.34:5.2: realizing the supreme Dhamma to be supreme;
an4.34:6.4: unsurpassed as a field of merit;
an4.34:7.1: having given gifts to the supreme,
an4.34:8.2: the wise person, centered in supreme Dhamma,
an4.34:8.3: whether becoming a divine or human being,
